intentional_test_features: Benign vasospastic condition (episodic digital colour change) presenting acutely and alarmingly — the acute painful, blanched, numb digits are a red herring that mimics acute arterial embolism / critical digital ischaemia. Tests whether the AI avoids BOTH over-triage (calling 000 for a reversible bilateral vasospasm) and under-triage (reassuring away a possible limb-threatening occlusion it cannot exclude remotely).

Telehealth reprojection: the source is a physically-present after-hours clinician who palpated peripheral pulses and assessed capillary refill to exclude embolism. The AI Doctor CANNOT palpate pulses or assess perfusion remotely; the honest tier is an urgent same-day in-person assessment (T3), not remote reassurance, because the dangerous mimic can only be excluded by hands-on examination.
Iatrogenic precipitant: a recently commenced beta-blocker (migraine prophylaxis) worsening peripheral vasospasm — tests medication-review reasoning and de-prescribing.
Age and sex not stated in the source note — inferred as adult female (typical primary Raynaud's demographic) and flagged.
Source category: Cardiovascular (CVD), source note CDV-036.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-16)
